name: Half marathon on the Camín Real de la Mesa — circular from Grado
name_original: Media maratón en el Camín Real de la Mesa — circular desde Grado
region_intro: Jogging around Grau/Grado offers diverse routes across its unique coastal landscapes and natural reserves. The region is characterized by extensive beaches, a significant lagoon, and accessible natural reserves, providing varied terrain for running. Much of the area features generally flat terrain, making it suitable for a wide range of fitness levels. Joggers can explore well-maintained pathways that combine seafront promenades with routes through the lagoon and surrounding nature. Best jogging routes around Grau/Grado The most popular jogging route is Half marathon on the Camín Real de la Mesa — circular from Grado, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging experience for experienced runners. Another top favourite among local runners is Río Cubia loop from Grau / Grado, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This shorter loop is ideal for a quick, refreshing run along the river. Local runners also love the Grado Town Center – Peñaflor Bridge loop from Grau / Grado, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through the town center and towards the Peñaflor Bridge, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Jogging around Grau/Grado is defined by coastal paths, serene lagoon views, and routes through natural reserves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy seafront runs to more challenging trails. The routes in Grau/Grado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Grau/Grado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running trails can I find in Grau/Grado?
A: Grau/Grado offers a diverse range of running trails, from easy coastal paths and seafront promenades to more challenging routes through natural reserves and the Grado Lagoon. The terrain is generally flat, making it accessible for various fitness levels, but some routes may include slight elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Grau/Grado?
A: Yes, Grau/Grado has several easy running options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cubia loop from Grau / Grado&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.2 km path ideal for a quick, refreshing run.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes in the Grau/Grado area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running paths in Grau/Grado are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cubia loop from Grau / Grado&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24579692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grado Town Center – Peñaflor Bridge loop from Grau / Grado&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 9.5 km run.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I might see while running in Grau/Grado?
A: While jogging in Grau/Grado, you can enjoy picturesque coastal views, the serene Grado Lagoon, and routes through natural reserves like Valle Cavanata. Some routes might also pass by historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Mary of Naranco&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1070132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel de Lillo&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by waterfalls in the region?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a route with a waterfall, you might explore areas that include natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guanga Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. These natural features can add a beautiful element to your running experience.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Grau/Grado?
A: The running routes in Grau/Grado are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 14 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from coastal paths to natural reserves, and the well-maintained surfaces that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in Grau/Grado?
A: Yes, the generally flat terrain and well-maintained paths, especially along the seafront promenades and cycle paths, make many routes suitable for families. Shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cubia loop from Grau / Grado&lt;/a&gt; are great for a family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Grau/Grado?
A: Grado is often called 'Sun Island,' and its south-facing beaches and mild climate make it suitable for running year-round. While summer offers warm weather for beach runs, the Lungomare seafront remains popular even in winter for serene jogs, offering a pleasant atmosphere without the summer crowds.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes for experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Grau/Grado offers longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056095352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Half marathon on the Camín Real de la Mesa — circular from Grado&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.1 km trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Degollada Recreation Area – Peñaflor Bridge loop from Grado&lt;/a&gt; is an even longer 24.1 km route, both providing a significant workout.

Q: How many running routes are available in Grau/Grado?
A: There are nearly 20 dedicated running routes available in Grau/Grado, catering to various skill levels and preferences. This includes 3 easy, 10 moderate, and 4 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any natural reserves accessible for running near Grau/Grado?
A: Yes, Grado is surrounded by significant natural reserves that are excellent for running. The Valle Cavanata Regional Nature Reserve and the Foce dell'Isonzo Natural Reserve offer unspoiled environments with rich flora and fauna, accessible via convenient cycle paths that can also be used for jogging.

Q: Where can I find more information about Grado as a destination?
A: For general tourist information about Grado, including details on accommodation, events, and other activities, you can visit the official tourism website: &lt;a href="http://www.grado-tourism.com/en/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;grado-tourism.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The Cubia River is a tributary of the Nalón River that runs through the municipality of Grado, in Asturias. It rises on the slopes of La Berza peak, in the Porcabezas mountain range, and runs for approximately 30.6 kilometres until it meets the Nalón.
The river is known for its picturesque landscapes and ecological importance. Along its course, you can find native forests, palaces, towers and traditional mills. In addition, the Cubia River route is ideal for hiking or cycling, offering an enriching experience for nature and history lovers.
From this small watchtower you can see Grado, council of Asturias. What is most striking, however, is the greenery of the vegetation.

Visiting Grado, in Asturias, is an enriching experience due to its rich history, culture and natural beauty.
Plaza General Ponte: The heart of Grado's commercial and social life, famous for its traditional market held on Wednesdays and Sundays.
Río Cubia: Ideal for walks or bike rides, with picturesque landscapes and routes that allow you to explore nature and local history.
Monasterio de San Salvador: An impressive Romanesque-style architectural complex in Cornellana, with a beautiful natural setting.
Palacio de Miranda-Valdecarzana: A historic building that stands out for its architecture and cultural importance.
Capilla de los Dolores: An 18th-century Baroque monument, known for its impressive architecture.
